frameworks.name: fMRI repetition suppression
frameworks.one_line: Repetition-suppression contrasts index neural sensitivity to transsaccadic feature changes.

frameworks.name: transsaccadic perception
frameworks.one_line: Continuity of object identity, orientation, and location across saccades.

frameworks.name: dorsal/ventral functional specialization
frameworks.one_line: Occipital vs parietal cortex divide labour for intrinsic vs extrinsic object properties.

core_question: Do occipital and parietal cortex jointly support transsaccadic discrimination of object shape and orientation?
key_assumptions: Repetition suppression indexes neural sensitivity to feature change
Shape and orientation are dissociable object properties
Saccades require active neural mechanisms to maintain perceptual continuity
key_findings: Cuneus and supramarginal gyrus form a cortical network engaged in transsaccadic discrimination
Cuneus is more sensitive to shape changes across the saccade
Supramarginal gyrus is more sensitive to orientation changes across the saccade
interpretations: Occipital and parietal cortex play complementary roles in transsaccadic perception of intrinsic versus extrinsic spatial properties
